problem.title: Missing Number
problem.description: Given an array nums containing n distinct numbers in the range [0, n], return the only number in the range that is missing from the array.
assumptions: nums contains n distinct numbers in range [0, n].

example.explanation: Array contains 0..3 except 2. Use sum formula: n*(n+1)/2 - sum(nums), or XOR.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – XOR [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: XOR every index i and every element nums[i] together, starting with n. Because XOR is self-inverse, every number that appears in both the index range and the array cancels out, leaving only the missing number. The key insight is that XOR-ing the complete set 0..n with all elements of nums eliminates all present numbers and isolates the absent one.

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Gauss Sum Formula [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use the arithmetic series formula: the sum of 0..n is n*(n+1)/2. Subtract the actual sum of nums from this expected sum — the difference is the missing number. This is the most intuitive approach and often what interviewers expect to hear first.
